rationale: The citing paper uses Manchanda et al. (2006) to position the prior banner-advertising literature as primarily focused on online outcomes, which motivates its own cross-channel contribution. It also cites Manchanda et al. when justifying a modeling choice, explicitly contrasting its setting with Manchanda's repeat-purchase context and therefore not modeling interpurchase time in the same way. These uses go beyond a passing mention and materially inform both framing and model specification.
relation_description: The citing study extends Manchanda et al. (2006) from online purchase effects of banner ads to a broader cross-channel setting, examining website visits and offline purchases across campaigns. It also adapts the empirical setup by contrasting and departing from Manchanda's interpurchase-time focus due different assortment dynamics.
